title: India's new Securities Code has powerful tools; The Jane Street case shows exactly why they may not be enough
description: India's Securities Markets Code Bill, 2025, has attracted significant commentary since its introduction in the Lok Sabha in December 2025. Most of it has been favourable, and some of it is warranted. Consolidating three statutes that were written across four different decades, unifying the definitional architecture, giving statutory backing to disgorgement, and introducing a limitation period for investigations, these are genuine improvements to a framework that had become increasingly difficult
